rationale: The cited paper appears only once in a grouped citation (with Khan and Dhar 2006; Mukhopadhyay and Johar 2009) to support a general statement that consumers use strategies to feel better about indulgent choices. The citing paper does not engage Okada (2005)'s specific hedonic-versus-utilitarian justification results, nor does it build hypotheses, measures, or analyses directly on that study. Its function here is broad background context on vice-consumption coping rather than a distinct intellectual dependency.
